compiled_state.title: [dso:sprint skill loader]: Skill invoked but content not injected into context -> orchestrator never executes sprint workflow
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: CHRONIC INTERMITTENT P0. In a host project session, invoking dso:sprint via the Skill tool returns 'Successfully loaded skill' but the skill content is never injected into the agent context. The orchestrator receives no instructions and falls back to asking the user what to do (offering manual orchestration, checking skill config, etc.). The skill call loop repeats 2-3 times with the same result. Root cause unknown — may be plugin cache miss, Skill tool timeout, or skill content exceeding injection limit. Requires maximum escalation: bot-psychologist + empirical reproduction in host project session.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 8 (ADVANCED) — severity=2, complexity=2, environment=2, intermittent=2. Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: behavioral ADVANCED (score 8). Gate 1a: skipped CLI_user
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Reclassified: llm-behavioral (dual-signal: agent behavioral drift + skill file involvement in using-lockpick/sprint SKILL.md)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: New related evidence from 2a75-5e96: Skill tool returns 'Launching skill: dso:sprint' (intermediate state only) with no follow-on content. session startup collision hypothesis: using-lockpick hook injected command-message block before skill loaded. Fix for 354b-7857 should cover this case.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix applied: (1) using-lockpick/SKILL.md — added injection verification + silent failure fallback + terse-string detection. (2) HOOK-INJECTION.md — same changes for consistency. (3) sprint/SKILL.md — added INJECTION_GUARD block after SUB-AGENT-GUARD. Also covers 2a75-5e96 (Launching skill terse string case).
comments.author: Test
